original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Coeliac disease: recognition, assessment and management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, young people, and children with suspected or confirmed coeliac disease, with age-specific testing and referral recommendations.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports symptom and risk review, clinician-reviewed serology, annual review domains, persistent-symptom context, and entered follow-up without interpreting results or generating diet, test, or referral actions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.4 — recognition, serology, and referral context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports symptoms, risk groups, gluten exposure context, and clinician-reviewed serology without automatic diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.4 — monitoring and annual review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports persistent symptoms, weight, height, dietary adherence discussion, complications, reviewed tests, and clinician-entered follow-up.

title: Coeliac disease: recognition, assessment and management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adults, young people, and children with suspected or confirmed coeliac disease, with age-specific testing and referral recommendations.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
